sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, amounts INTEGER[], created_at TIMESTAMP);
SELECT id, to_weeks(SUM(amounts[1])) AS week_interval FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING to_weeks(SUM(amounts[1])) && [to_weeks(1), to_weeks(2)] IS TRUE;
SELECT id, to_weeks(COALESCE(amounts[1], 0)) AS week_interval FROM test_data WHERE amounts[1] IS NOT NULL OR created_at IS NULL ORDER BY week_interval DESC;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE NULL && amounts IS TRUE GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE to_weeks((SELECT MAX(amounts[1]) FROM test_data) + 5) && [to_weeks(2), to_weeks(NULL)] IS TRUE;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_data AS SELECT id, to_weeks(amounts[1] + 1) AS week_interval FROM test_data WHERE amounts[1] IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id FROM test_data t1 JOIN temp_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t2.week_interval && [to_weeks(3), to_weeks(4)] AND t1.created_at IS NOT NULL;
